QF.id: Registration, Code of Conduct and General Obligations_54

question.text: Under the Amendment Regulations 2025, a merchant banker wishing to carry out non-SEBI regulated activities must do so through:

answer.text: A joint venture with an NBFC
Strategic Business Units (SBUs) with proper segregation and ring-fencing
Direct operations under the main merchant banking license

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Non-SEBI regulated activities must be conducted through Strategic Business Units (SBUs) that ensure proper segregation and ring-fencing from the core merchant banking operations. **The Trap:** A separate subsidiary is not required — SBUs within the same entity are sufficient, provided they maintain proper segregation. Direct operations without ring-fencing would violate the regulations. **Cross-Exam Context:** Ring-fencing concepts are tested across NISM modules dealing with intermediary regulations, including Series VI (Depository Operations). **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** When setting up SBUs, ensure documentation clearly defines the boundaries — regulatory audits focus heavily on whether ring-fencing is genuine or merely cosmetic.
